themes: model quality vs. rate limits tradeoff

worthClicking.author: @bcherny
worthClicking.why: Anthropic's own product lead confirms Opus 4.7 uses more thinking tokens and they've increased rate limits to compensate — directly relevant for anyone pricing AI tooling.

worthClicking.author: @benhylak
worthClicking.why: High-engagement contrarian take (2457 likes, 200k views) that Opus 4.7 is a regression — worth reading the replies to calibrate whether the launch is a trust problem.

worthClicking.author: @ThisWeeknAI
worthClicking.why: Side-by-side 4.6 vs. 4.7 design comparison with 315k views is the kind of concrete before/after that matters for AI product quality claims.

worthClicking.author: @GergelyOrosz
worthClicking.why: Flags a real enterprise budget crunch: as Anthropic removes subsidies, AI coding tools risk hitting a $300/month ceiling — directly relevant for sales enablement and pricing strategy.

worthClicking.author: @TechCrunch
worthClicking.why: Anthropic CPO leaving Figma's board over a competing product signals Anthropic is moving directly into design tooling, which reshapes the competitive landscape.

worthClicking.author: @aakashgupta
worthClicking.why: Factory's 5x valuation jump to $1.5B in 7 months is framed as a bet that model lock-in is not a moat — a sharp read on AI coding product strategy.

worthClicking.author: @businessbarista
worthClicking.why: Articulates the enterprise knowledge context problem clearly: coding is solved because git has the context, but knowledge work doesn't — points at a real product gap.

worthClicking.author: @GergelyOrosz
worthClicking.why: Predicts 'UX-pilled' builders will be in massive demand as AI levels the technical playing field — directly relevant for distribution and indie dev positioning.

worthClicking.author: @aakashgupta
worthClicking.why: Spots the unusual move in Anthropic's launch chart where Mythos Preview beats Opus 4.7 on every benchmark — raises real questions about what Opus 4.7 is actually for.

voices.why: Consistently grounding AI hype in enterprise budget and product reality — two posts this cycle both directly on-topic.
Sharp structural takes on AI product strategy and funding signals; multiple posts this cycle with real analytical depth.
Thinking clearly about the enterprise knowledge layer problem, which is an emerging product category worth tracking.
Inside signal from Anthropic's product team on rate limits and token economics.

examples: Elon/Pope/Iran political dunks with no product relevance
vague AI hype threads with no specifics ("most powerful prompt ever", "learning mode BREAKING")
